name: Mujammi ibn Abdullah
title: Guided by Tirimmah
shortDescription: A warrior from Kufa who, along with his son Aa'idh and guided by Tirimmah, joined the Imam's caravan at Udhayb al-Hijanat.
fullStory:
Mujammi ibn Abdullah al-A'idhi was a sincere Shia and well-known warrior from Kufa. Having deep devotion to the Prophet's (PBUH) family, he was waiting for an opportunity to help Imam Hussain (AS).

When the situation became difficult for Shias in Kufa, Mujammi, along with his son 'Aa'idh' and several others including Amr ibn Khalid al-Saydawi, decided to secretly leave Kufa. Guided by Tirimmah ibn Uday, who knew the deserts, they reached the Imam's caravan at the area of 'Udhayb al-Hijanat'.

There, Hurr ibn Yazid al-Riyahi tried to prevent them from joining or arrest them, but Imam Hussain (AS) firmly stated: 'These are my companions and I will defend them.'

On the day of Ashura, Mujammi ibn Abdullah, along with his fellow travelers, launched a collective attack into the heart of the enemy army. They were surrounded by the Kufans and fought manfully. After Hazrat Abbas (AS) temporarily rescued them, they returned to the battlefield.

Finally, after a heroic battle, Mujammi ibn Abdullah was martyred by sword strikes, immortalizing his name as one of the true companions of the Master of Martyrs.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى مُجَمِّعِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ العائِذِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Mujammi ibn Abdullah al-A'idhi.
